nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sancto
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: nickname from Latin sanctus ‘holy, sainted’, denoting a pious person. Compare Saint .

variants.name: Saint
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

nameMeanings.name: Albino
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Ramon, Manuel, Ana, Carlos, Julio, Luis, Raul, Ruben, Alfonso, Andres, Angel.  Italian Pasquale, Angelo, Antonio, Carmine, Giovanni, Luigi, Sal, Santo, Saverio, Ubalda.
Portuguese, Spanish, and southern Italian:
from the personal name Albino (see Albin ).

variants.name: Albini
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Antonio, Pasquale, Aldo, Amalio, Americo, Armando, Carlo, Caterina, Claudio, Gino, Mario, Maurizio, Nichola, Nicola, Severino, Vittorio.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of   Albino  .

variants.name: Albin
meanings: English, French, and German: from the personal name Albin (from Latin Albinus, a derivative of albus ‘white’). In England this is generally a variant of Alban . The usual spelling of the French name is Aubin . The personal name was especially popular in Austria, Lombardy, and Savoy, where it absorbed the ancient Germanic name Albuin (which is composed of the elements alb ‘elf’ + win ‘friend’; compare Alvin ). This was the name of the Lombard leader (died 572) who made himself king of northern Italy, and also of various Christian saints, including a bishop of Brixen (Bressanone) in South Tyrol.
Galician (Albín): habitational name from any of the four places called Albín in Galicia (Spain), from Latin (villa) Albini.

variants.name: Alvino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Pietro, Antonio, Ciro, Salvatore, Savino.  Spanish Sabino, Aurelio, Mario, Elvira, Genoveva, Gerardo, Gilberto, Guillermo, Lazaro, Luz Maria, Margarito, Maria Carmen.
Italian (Campania): from the personal name Alvino, which is of ancient Germanic origin. Alternatively, it may perhaps be a variant of Albino .

nameMeanings.name: Mauger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French and Channel Islands (Guernsey and Jersey): from the Old French (Norman) personal name Maug(i)er, Malg(i)er, from ancient Germanic Madalgar(i), Malger, composed of the elements madal ‘council, meeting’ and gar(i) ‘spear’, or from Amalgar(i), with the first element amal ‘capable, brave, eager’. Compare Major , Monger , and Munger .
Probably also an altered form of German   Mager  .

variants.name: Maugeri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Carmine, Ignazio, Salvatore, Angelo, Camillo, Giuseppe, Orazio, Pietro.
Italian (southern):
variant of Malgeri, from the ancient Germanic personal name Madalgari or Amalgari (see Mauger ).

variants.name: Major
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Malg(i)er, Maug(i)er, an Old French form of ancient Germanic Madalgar(i) (see Mauger ).
Hungarian: from a shortened form of majorosgazda (see Majoros ) or a derivative of German Meyer .
Polish, Czech, and Slovak: from the military rank major (derived from Latin maior ‘greater’), a word related to English mayor and the German surname Meyer .

variants.name: Mangieri
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Gaspar, Mario, Angelo, Arsenio, Mafalda, Orazio, Rocco, Silvio.
Italian: variant of Malgeri, from the ancient Germanic personal name Madalgari or Amalgari (see Mauger ).

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albino married Mauger de Sancto Albino before 1220, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 son.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albino a épousé Mauger de Sancto Albino avant 1220, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albino hat vor 1220 in England Mauger de Sancto Albino geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albino lei sposato Mauger de Sancto Albino prima del 1220, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio.

ja.Henstridge, Somerset, England, United Ki: イギリスイギリスソメルセトヘンストリッジ
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albinoは1220年以前にイギリスでMauger de Sancto Albinoと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albino(은)는 1220년 이전 영국에서 Mauger de Sancto Albino(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albino casou-se com Mauger de Sancto Albino antes 1220, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albino вышла замуж за человека по имени Mauger de Sancto Albino перед 1220 г. в местности Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Mauger de Sancto Albino se casó con Mauger de Sancto Albino antes de 1220, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo.
